title: ardisia esquipulasana
intro: Ardisia esquipulasana, also known as Guayabal, is a plant species in the Ardisia genus, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Ardisia esquipulasana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Ardisia esquipulasana can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water, which is then consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
